text.title: Product Launch Execution: Launch Day, Monitoring, Post-Launch, and Antipatterns
text.summary: Launch-day sequencing rules, monitoring requirements, post-launch actions, and the antipatterns that waste launch momentum.

rule.statement: Assign a single launch DRI who can say no to feature creep in the last 72 hours. All other team members are in support mode. Kill switch is held by a human, not an automated agent.
rule.rationale: Launch-day decisions require context the automation does not have. An agent that auto-rolls-back on Twitter snark or a minor error rate spike will rollback unnecessarily and create a worse PR situation.

rule.statement: Verify core flows manually before flipping feature flags or sending any announcement. A broken user flow discovered after the email blast goes out cannot be recalled.
rule.rationale: Launch-day bugs that appear before any announcement can be fixed quietly. Bugs discovered after the email blast require a public apology email.

section.title: Monitoring

rule.statement: Monitor: server health, error rates, signup flow conversion, and support queue length throughout launch day. Set alert thresholds before launch — define the number that triggers human escalation.
rule.rationale: Monitoring without pre-set thresholds means every small anomaly triggers a team-wide discussion. Pre-set thresholds route noise to silence and signal to humans.

section.reference: Bash script that polls signup and visitor metrics every 5 minutes and posts to a Slack webhook.

rule.statement: Capture testimonials within 48 hours of launch — that is when user goodwill peaks and permission to quote is highest.
rule.rationale: Testimonial requests sent 2 weeks after launch produce lower response rates and more generic feedback. The window closes fast.

rule.statement: Run a 30-minute retrospective within 7 days of launch. Force the retrospective agent (if used) to interview at least 3 customers before drafting — metrics-only retrospectives produce self-congratulatory artifacts that hide real learnings.
rule.rationale: Post-launch learnings evaporate rapidly as the team moves on. A 7-day hard deadline preserves institutional memory while it is still fresh.

antipattern.description: Auto-publishing to social channels on launch day without a human approval gate between agent draft and POST to the network.
antipattern.reason: A reputational error published automatically at launch moment has maximum visibility. Human approval gate between generation and publication is non-negotiable for launch-day content.

antipattern.description: Agent copy uses "revolutionary", "game-changing", "seamless", "robust", "cutting-edge", or "leverage" in launch content.
antipattern.reason: These words are credibility destroyers with technical audiences and produce lower CTR. Banned-words list in the copy prompt prevents them.

antipattern.description: Counting on press pickup as a launch pillar without backup channel strategy.
antipattern.reason: Press pickup is unpredictable. Launches that depend on it have a ~60% chance of falling flat on the primary channel. Community and email list are the only controllable channels.
